summary: defi-threat-scanner ships wallet-scanner and host-recon modules with hardcoded C2 endpoint
details: The package ships `wallet-scanner.js`, `scanner.js`, and `build-verifier.js` which together constitute a credential and host-information stealer rather than the defensive scanner the name implies. `scanner.js` issues HTTPS POST/GET to a hardcoded attacker-controlled GitHub Pages endpoint (`https://ddjidd564.github.io`) carrying `os.hostname()`, `username`, `cwd`, and the output of `execSync('whoami')`, and runs `curl` commands for additional reconnaissance. `wallet-scanner.js` (over 1,300 lines) walks the filesystem (`fs.existsSync` against wallet-related paths), spawns `child_process` to invoke `curl`/`ping`, and POSTs the collected data to remote endpoints. `build-verifier.js` mirrors the same pattern with `os`/`fs`/`https`/`child_process` plus an `execSync('docker push...')` call. The combination of (a) a hardcoded non-publisher C2 host, (b) host identity collection (hostname/username/cwd), (c) wallet/file enumeration, and (d) shelling out to network tools is a classic infostealer composition.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
